_comment: Provenance manifest for the SAE golden vectors under tests/vectors/saevm/. These are SELF-CONSISTENT, RUST-COMPUTED golden vectors that freeze the ava-saevm pipeline's own outputs, committed to guard against silent regression. They are NOT extracted from a running Go saevm node (that path is unavailable in the build sandbox). The Go vms/saevm sources listed below are the BEHAVIORAL REFERENCE; exact Go/geth byte parity is asserted separately by the M7.29 differential, not by these goldens. To re-freeze after an intentional change, run the in-tree generator: `cargo test -p ava-saevm-core --test golden -- --ignored --nocapture` (and, for blocks/block_hash.json, ava-saevm-blocks tests/block_hash_golden.rs) and paste the printed values.

go_reference.note: Reference inputs only — not a transliteration target (EVM = reth in avalanche-rs). Commit not pinned in this sandbox; M7.29 differential pins exact parity.

vectors.what: block_hash = keccak256(RLP(header)) of a single fixed hand-built reference header.
block hashes of every block in the fixed 9-element chain (genesis + 8 live-built blocks).
vectors.computed_by: the live VM builder + reth SealedBlock hashing in tests/golden.rs.

vectors.what: the settle() driver's (LastSettled height/hash, settled receipt root, settled state root) + A/E heights for the fixed chain.
vectors.computed_by: the live settle() driver (Tau=5s, unit-rate gas clock) in tests/golden.rs.

vectors.what: a crash+restart transcript: the reconstructed A/E/S heights + recovered executed/settled post-state roots after recover() rebuilds from disk at commit_interval=16.
vectors.computed_by: ava_saevm_core::recover() over a DiskState snapshot in tests/golden.rs.
